title: International screenings of 30
wikitext:
[]: International screenings of 3.0?             Quote Post by JRBradley » Thu Jul 12, 2012 3:46 am Hi guys, long time reader here, first time poster. Anyways, basically I'm just wondering if anyone who has been in the fandom for a while around here or just generally has a better grasp of knowledge than I, are there ever usually any screenings of the Rebuild films outside of Japan around/shortly after the time they are released? Like in major cities such as New York, London, Sydney, Paris etc? Or is usually just limited to a theatrical run in Japan then screenings at festivals? Reason for this being is that I'll be in the US at the end of the year/start of next and I'm just hoping that I'll be lucky. Thanks!

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Jul 12, 2012 4:58 am Its true that the past Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 films have appeared at some International Film Festivals around the world. 2.0 for example had its international premiere in Hawaii. And I do believe 1.0 and 2.0 also had screenings at the Venice International Film Festival. It is quite possible that If Anno and his studio have completed enough animation that an early showing for Eva 3.0 may even occur sometime in August or September. Two rather large and reknown international film festivals are happening in August and September. The Venice International Film Festival and Toronto International Film Festival. I live in Toronto and currently keeping my fingers crossed that Eva 3.0 will come here in September. Two high profile anime films came to Toronto's International Film Festival last year. A Letter to Momo (Production IG) and From up on Poppy Hill (Studio Ghibli). What was interesting was that the former, "A Letter to Momo" had its international premiere here and we got to see it 7 months before Japanese audiences would ever see it. Done by high profile anime studio Production IG, I'm sure Studio Khara would have taken notice that two high profile anime studios have visited Toronto last year and that this year the city would be the place to go to showcase their newest Eva film. Fingers crossed!!! :) Ha as for your luck.... Yes its true that you may lucky if your in the right place at the right time but more often than not as a possible film festival attendee, you have to plan ahead of time. Anything film festival screening will require you to buy your tickets ahead of time. And if you are in the US you may only be there for a limited time. Its best to see instead whether a Eva 3.0 will arrive in your home country for a international screening rather than hoping for it to be their at your vacation/business destination.

[]:             Quote Post by Knuckles » Thu Jul 12, 2012 5:04 am 2.0 came out in Japanese theaters in June 2009, and released in North American theaters in January 2011. It took a couple months later to get to my area, and I saw it in theaters in March 2011. (they were with subtitles, if you were curious) so it took a year and a half for us to see it over here with even just the subbed version. we can expect something like that for 3.0, unless they've taken the time to properly plan an upcoming international release in a reasonable time frame this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Thu Jul 12, 2012 5:24 am Knuckles wrote: so it took a year and a half for us to see it over here with even just the subbed version. we can expect something like that for 3.0, unless they've taken the time to properly plan an upcoming international release in a reasonable time frame this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Jul 12, 2012 5:27 am Knuckles wrote: so it took a year and a half for us to see it over here with even just the subbed version. we can expect something like that for 3.0, unless they've taken the time to properly plan an upcoming international release in a reasonable time frame this time around.

[]:             Quote Post by JRBradley » Thu Jul 12, 2012 6:26 am Thanks for the replies everyone! Electric Sachiel wrote: Its best to see instead whether a Eva 3.0 will arrive in your home country for a international screening rather than hoping for it to be their at your vacation/business destination.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Jul 12, 2012 9:32 am Will be down to Melbourne with the quickness if Reel Anime screen it next year... Fun fact: I'm actually going to be in Japan, 16-25 November. My Japanese is really poor though, so probably not worth going to see it in the cinemas whilst there. It'd probably just be confusing as hell. Paradox. Also, moving to Canada - Good choice man! ;)

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Jul 13, 2012 2:59 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: I live in Toronto and currently keeping my fingers crossed that Eva 3.0 will come here in September.

[]:             Quote Post by Josh_LawLiet » Fri Jul 13, 2012 4:55 pm At year 2009 I lived similar experience to now. Strong World Film (One Piece) was released at 2009 november ot december, then my surprise was that in barcelona, where I live, it was released at special event (at 1 theatre) only FEW WEEKS after official and with spanish subs. Hoping lots that happends same with Eva 3.0.

[]:             Quote Post by JRBradley » Sat Jul 14, 2012 9:38 am Blue Monday wrote: Will be down to Melbourne with the quickness if Reel Anime screen it next year... Fun fact: I'm actually going to be in Japan, 16-25 November. My Japanese is really poor though, so probably not worth going to see it in the cinemas whilst there. It'd probably just be confusing as hell. Paradox. Also, moving to Canada - Good choice man! ;)
